Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 023 [Šu-du₈-a][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 10-KAM U₄.DA-KAM ŠU.DU₈.A-KAM

(o 1) In Ayyaru (Month II), the 10th day is the fixed date for the hand removing.

o 22

mul₂ABSIN ša₂ MUL₂.MUL₂ gišESI U₂.NAM.TI.LA ina KUŠ GU₄

(o 2) The Furrow (then stands in) the Bristle. You put ebony (and) the life-giving-plant in a (bag of) ox skin; you wrap it up in the ox skin. You thread a sankallu-stone, ... of ebony (and) a pellet of adāru-wood on a band of black wool; you wrap around (it) huppu-wrappings of black wool;

o 33

GARan ina KUŠ GU₄ GAG.GAGpi₂ na₄SAG.KAL ḪA ša₂ gišESI ku-pi-tu₄ ša₂ gišILDAG₂

o 44

ina DUR SIG₂ GE₆ it-ti me-eli E₃ lip₂-pi ḫup₂-pi ša₂ SIG₂ GE₆ tala₂-pap

o 55

ina GU₂-šu₂ GARan gišESI U₂.NAM.TI.LA na₄SAG.KAL ina I₃.GIŠ GI DU₁₀.GA ŠEŠ₂

(o 5) you put (it) around his neck; you anoint the ebony, the life-giving-plant, the sankallu-stone with sweet reed oil.

o 66

EN₂ šimli numun ašag sig₁₀-ga EN₂ e-piš lem-nu 2ta EN₂-MEŠ an-na-a-tu₂

(o 6) Incantation: "Juniper, filling the field with seeds!"; incantation:"Evil sorceror"; you recite these two incantations 6 times on top (of it). Let the man wash himself. Let him cleanse himself. Let him annoint (himself with) sweet reed oil.

o 77

6-TA.AM₃ ana UGU ŠIDnu LU₂ lim-te-si ḪE₂.DADAG I₃ GI DU₁₀.GA ŠEŠ₂

o 88

kušE.SIR₂ GAR NINDA šeEŠTUB GU₇ KAŠ ZU₂.LUM.MA NAG UZU GU₄ UDU u MUŠEN GU₇ ina KI.NU₂ KI MUNUS ḪE₂.NU₂

(o 8) Let him put sandals (on). Let him eat arsuppu-bread. Let him drink suluppu-date beer. Let him eat beef, mutton and poultry. Let him lie down in bed with a woman.

o 99

di-nu NU ŠE.GA ana gišKIRI₆ la₃ E₁₁ DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂ ana MUNUS.ḪUL GUB-MEŠ-šu₂ <ana> MUNUS.SIG₅ GUB-MEŠ-šu₂

(o 9) Lawsuits will not be favourable. He must not go up to the garden. May the gods who stood (by) him for evil stand (by) him for goodness!

o 1010

EN₂ an-zu-u₂ u₂-ba-ʾa an-zu-u₂ a-sak-ku

(o 10) Incantation: "I seek Anzu, Anzu the asakku-demon!

o 1111

man-nu ana u₂-ru an-zi-i lu-še-la-an-ni

(o 11) Who will help me climb up onto Anzu's roof?

o 1212

ana TUR₃ an-zi-i lu-še-rib-an-ni

(o 12) (Who) will let me enter into Anzu's animal stall?

o 1313

liš-kun ana ŠU-MIN-ia rit-ti an-zi-i še-bi-ti u ṭa-ḫu-ut-ti

(o 13) May he put the sated and ample claw of Anzu in my hands!"

o 1414

ana INIM-šu₂ i-pu-ul ba-ni-ti re-ʾi-in-du DINGIR-MEŠ

(o 14) The most beloved of the gods answered his request (with) favourable words:

o 1515

al-ki NENNI₂ A NENNI₂ ana UR₃ an-zi-i lu-še-li-ka

(o 15) "Go, so-and-so son of so-and-so! Let me help you climb up onto Anzu's roof!

o 1616

ana TUR₃ an-zi-i lu-še-rib-ka

(o 16) Let me help you enter Anzu's animal stall!

o 1717

lu-uš-kun ana ŠU-MIN-ka rit-ti an-zi-i še-bi-ti u ṭa-ḫu-ut-ti te EN₂

(o 17) Let me place the sated and ample claw of Anzu into your hands!" Incantation formula.

o 1818

KA.INIM.MA šu duḫ-a-kam

(o 18) Wording of hand removing.

o 1919

DU₃.DU₃-BI ana UGU rit-ti an-zi-i EN₂ an-nit 7šu₂ ŠIDnu-ma

(o 19) Its ritual: you recite 7 times this incantation on top of Anzu's claw and you take (the claw) in your hands and they will give you anything you wish.

o 2020

ina ŠU-MIN-ka DAB-ma mim-ma ter-ri-šu₂!(ŠI) i-nam-di-nu-ka

o 2121

EN₂ UŠUMGAL ANe u KIti₃ at-ta-ma

(o 21) Incantation:"You are the great dragon of heaven and earth!"

o 2222

ZALAG₂ DI KU ḫi-pi₂dUTU ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂

(o 22) Light, ... (gloss: break) Šamaš (gloss: new break).

o 2323

ap-lu gaš-ru i-lit-ti dŠEŠ.KIri

(o 23) Strong son, offspring of Nannaru, wise (god), I call you! Šamaš (gloss: new break).

o 2424

en-qu al-si-ka dUTU ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂

o 2525

mim-ma er-ri-šu₂!(ŠI) si-lim

(o 25) Be at peace with all that I wish!

o 2626

lid-di-nu <<NU>> a-na ia-a-ši te EN₂

(o 26) May they give (that) to me!" Incantation formula.

Reverse
r 1r 1

KA.INIM.MA šu duḫ-a-kam

(r 1) Wording of hand removing:

r 22

DU₃.DU₃-BI gišKIŠIB.LA₂ gišTAŠKARIN pa-ṭir-ti DU₃uš an-nit 7šu₂

(r 2) Its ritual: you make an open claw of boxwood; you recite this 7 times in front of Šamaš on top (of it) and you put (it) around his neck.

r 33

ana IGI dUTU a-na UGU ŠIDnu ina GU₂-šu₂ GARan

r 44

EN₂ lu₂ niŋ₂-nam dug₄-ga ḫe₂-še-ga

(r 4) Incantation: "May everything the man says be favourable!

r 55

lu₂ ka-inim-ma ta-e₃

(r 5) May the incantation release the man!

r 66

ḫe₂-sud? gi-gub-gub-ba silim-ma ḫe₂-še-ga

(r 6) May it go away! May it reach (the full extent of) a measuring rod! May it be favourable!

r 77

sig-bi dlamma silim-ma

(r 7) lamma-deity, make the weak one healthy!

r 88

mu-un-du-du ḫe₂-še-ga

(r 8) He goes. May it be favourable!

r 99

dug₄-ga dasar-lu₂-ḫi dutu maḫ te EN₂

(r 9) At the word of Asarluhi (and) Utu the great!". Incantation formula.

r 1010

KA.INIM.MA šu duḫ-a-kam

(r 10) Wording for the hand removing.

r 1111

DU₃.DU₃-BI na₄ZA.GIN₃ ina DUR SIG₂ BABBAR E₃-ma 7 KEŠ₂ KEŠ₂.KEŠ₂ e-ma KEŠ₂

(r 11) Its ritual: you thread lapis-lazuli on a band of white wool and you tie 7 knots; whenever you tie (a knot), you recite the incantation. You bind it at his right hand (lit. hands).

r 1212

EN₂ ŠIDnu ina ŠU-MIN! 15-šu₂ tara-kas₂

r 1313

10 GIN₂ gišal-la-an 1/2-šu₂ ina I₃.GIŠ EN₂ 7šu₂ ŠIDnu1

(r 13) You (...) half of 10 shekels of oak in oil; you recite the incantation 7 times.

r 1414

IGI-ka ŠEŠ₂aš <1/2-šu₂ ina> giSAG.KU₅ GI DU₁₀.GA EN₂ 7šu₂ ŠIDnu2

(r 14) You anoint your eyes; half of it a sweet reed stem; you recite the incantation 7 times.

r 1515

ina GABA-ka GARan

(r 15) You place (it) on your chest.

r 1616

rit-ti an-zi-i ša₂ bil-la-a-ti DU₃uš šum₄-ma bil-la-a-ti la ti-du-u₂

(r 16) You make Anzu's claw of alloy. If you do not know alloy: 7 grains of silver, 7 grains of gold, 7 grains of copper, 7 grains of lead, 7 grains of tin, 7 grains of iron (is) the total (for) the claw of Anzu. You make two-thirds of 1 shekel; you recite the incantation: "Anzu, I seek Anzu" on top (of them). You thread (them) on a band of blue-green wool (together) with 7 hands of wax.

r 1717

7 ŠE KU₃.BABBAR 7 ŠE KU₃.SI₂₂ 7 ŠE URUDU 7 ŠE A.GAR₅ 7 ŠE an-na-ku 7 ŠE AN.BAR

r 1818

PAP rit-ti an-zi-i 2ta ŠU-MIN-MEŠ ša 1 GIN₂ DU₃uš EN₂ an-zu-u₂ u₂-ba-ʾa an-zu-u₂

r 1919

7šu₂ ana muḫ-ḫi ŠIDnu ina DUR sig₂ZA.GIN₃.NA KI 7 ŠU-MIN-MEŠ ša₂ DUḪ.LAL₃ E₃3

r 2020

ana muḫ-ḫi ŠU-MIN-MEŠ ša₂ DUḪ.LAL₃ EN₂ en en ŠIDnu

(r 20) On top of the hands of wax, you recite the incantation: "Lord! Lord!".

r 2121

EN₂ en en en en en en en lu₂kiŋ₂-gi₄-a niŋ₂ al di šu šum₂-mu te EN₂

(r 21) Incantation: "Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Messenger! Give me all I wish". Incantation formula.

r 2222

ana muḫ-ḫi ša₂ iš-tu₄ GU₂-šu₂ i-de-ek-ka-aš₂ kam DU₁₁.GA

(r 22) He speaks thus on top of (the things) he has removed from his neck:

r 2323

EN₂ en en en en en en en lu₂kiŋ₂-gi₄-a nu šu šum₂-mu te EN₂

(r 23) Incantation: "Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Lord! Messenger! Do not entrust!". Incantation formula.

r 2424

ne₂-peš an-nu-u₂ ša₂ rit-ti an-zi-i ana KA ša₂-ṭir

(r 24) This (is) the ritual of Anzu's claw; written by dictation.

1One verb is probably between I₃.GIŠ and EN₂: editor had suggested tanaddi.

2Another verb is missing between DU₁₀.GA and EN₂: editor suggests taštanatti.

3There may be another object missing either between ŠID{+nu} and ina, or between KI and 7.

Created by Marie-Françoise Besnier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2011 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348628
